data.title: RoboSense sells 303,000 LiDAR units in 2025, a 1,142% increase from previous year, ranking No. 1.
data.description: In Shenzhen, China, on January 15, 2026, RoboSense (stock symbol: 2498.HK), a leader in AI-driven robotics technology, revealed that it sold 303,000 LiDAR units for robotics in 2025, making it the top seller in the industry*. This marks a remarkable 1,141.8% increase compared to the previous year.

data.title: Dogecoin down 3% with 9.60% drop in trading volume. Analyst predicts 30% upside if resistance is broken.
data.description: On Wednesday, Dogecoin (CRYPTO: DOGE) experienced a slight decline, despite an analyst pointing out a bullish technical pattern indicating significant potential for growth. The memecoin, known for its dog theme, saw a decrease of over 3%, accompanied by a 9.60% drop in trading volume within the last day. This downturn was more pronounced compared to other major cryptocurrencies like Ethereum (CRYPTO: ETH), XRP (CRYPTO: XRP), and Solana (CRYPTO: SOL). The market setback occurred as discussions on cryptocurrency market structure legislation faced another delay, affecting federal regulatory efforts. For more details, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Successful cadaver dissection class convened in Thailand with leading experts from Asia-Pacific countries at "Moyom Biotech • Aphranel Global Expert Advisory Panel • Thailand Anatomy Exchange Conference."
data.description: On January 14, 2026, the first-ever "Moyom Biotech • Aphranel Global Expert Advisory Panel • Thailand Anatomy Exchange Conference" took place in Bangkok. This significant event brought together experts from various Asia-Pacific nations. The conference aimed to facilitate the exchange of knowledge and expertise among professionals in the field, with a focus on anatomical sciences and biotechnology.

data.title: UK CfD AR7 awarded a record 8.4 GW of offshore wind capacity across 8 projects, signaling new economic realities.
data.description: In Denmark, the UK government announced the outcomes of Contracts for Difference Allocation Round 7 (AR7), showing a continuous interest in UK offshore wind and the challenges of submitting cost-efficient, viable bids. The allocation round assigned a historic 8.4 gigawatts of offshore wind capacity among eight projects, following a budget boost by the government. The majority of the capacity (8.2 GW) was granted to the aforementioned projects.

data.title: INJ price predicted to reach $6.20 in February with short-term target at $5.90 and critical support at $5.02. Bullish setup.
data.description: The predicted prices for INJ are as follows: a short-term target of $5.90 within the next week, a medium-term forecast ranging between $6.00 and $6.20 over the next month. The bullish breakout level is set at $5.90, while critical support is noted at $5.02.

data.title: Calavo Growers surges 13% in after-hours trading to $25.50 on merger news with Mission Produce acquiring them for $27 per share.
data.description: Calavo Growers Inc. (NASDAQ: CVGW) experienced a significant increase of 12.93% in after-hours trading, reaching $25.50, subsequent to the reveal of its latest financial outcomes and the announcement of a merger pact with Mission Produce Inc. (NASDAQ: AVO) headquartered in California. The agreed terms of the merger state that Mission Produce will purchase Calavo Growers at $27 per share, established on Mission's 30-trading-day volume-weighted average price concluding on Jan. 13. As part of the deal, Calavo shareholders will receive $14.85 in cash as well as 0.9790 shares of Mission for each Calavo share they hold. Post the finalization of the agreement, Mission Produce stakeholders will possess around 80.3% of the merged entity, with Calavo Growers shareholders retaining about 19.7%. data.title: BitsLab audited over 200 projects in 2025, discovering 2,858 vulnerabilities, ensuring Web3 infrastructure security.
data.description: In San Francisco on January 15, 2026, it was noted that the year 2025 witnessed significant advancements in Web3 infrastructure. One of the key players in this evolution, BitsLab, has set a precedent in AI-driven digital asset security. The company has been instrumental in ensuring the safety of Web3 infrastructure by conducting audits, penetration testing, and ongoing security surveillance. As of now, the exact number of audits conducted or the extent of security monitoring efforts were not specified.
